summary: STALE / substantively-fixed. EXP-253 c1 H1 (3.6× envelope wording): body text now uses explicit-arithmetic phrasing "is roughly 3.6× the rule's ±3 nT half-width (|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6), well outside the dome's own falsification window" — matches curmudgeon recommendation exactly. EXP-253 c1 H2 (strawman→treated-as-zero): replacement applied — current text says "the globe's actual prediction has been treated as zero in the dome's published rule" (one of three curmudgeon-recommended replacements). TLDR retains the older "3.6× outside the ±3 nT "globe" window" phrasing in <summary> tags but per analyst TLDR-review rule (fix factual errors, not nuance) this is acceptable — the math is correct under reading 1 (3.6 = 10.9/3), and the expanded detail carries the explicit-arithmetic version. Recommend close. Optional follow-up: TLDR-only polish if a future SEC-4.2.2 review wants it.
verification: grep "3.6× the rule's ±3 nT half-width" → 1 match; "replaced with a strawman" → 0 matches; "treated as zero" → 1 match in correct context.
